title: senecio neomexicanus var. mutabilis
intro: Senecio neomexicanus var. mutabilis, also known as the New Mexico ragwort or Gray's ragwort, is a plant species within the Senecio genus that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Senecio neomexicanus var. mutabilis include relieving coughs and congestion, reducing fever, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
